question: For cast-in-situ piles, the minimum reinforcement should be 0.40% of the sectional area calculated on the basis of outside area of the casing or shaft. The reinforcement can be curtailed along the depth of pile depending upon the nature of soil. In soft clay or loose sands, the reinforcement is to be provided upto the full pile depth. The minimum clear distance between two main reinforcements shall be 100 mm. The minimum diameter of the links or spirals shall be 6 mm and the spacing not less than 150 mm. 14.22  Retaining Walls The retaining walls are used to retain earth fill or any other material so that the ground surfaces at different elevations are maintained on either side of the retaining wall. These are mostly used for roads in hilly areas, swimming pools, at the end of bridges in the form of abutments, and under ground water tanks. The various types of retaining walls are as follows: Toe Heel Stem Heel Stem Backfill
